bindings.quest_action: hunt down the raiders and bring them to justice
bindings.quest_focus: the ransacked storehouse where the watchman died
bindings.quest_stakes: the memory of an innocent man who deserved better
bindings.quest_threat: the raiders who murdered the night watchman

dialogueChunks.intro: Sit down, {player_name}. What I'm about to tell you will make your blood boil, and I need you thinking clearly.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_origin}, a band of {enemy_type} hit {quest_focus} and killed the night watchman: an old man who never hurt anyone in his life. They took every last crate of {quest_item} and left him bleeding in the doorway. {target_npc} found the body at dawn. We tracked {quest_threat} {location_hint}, but the guards won't pursue beyond the boundary. Someone needs to {quest_action}.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_stakes} demands it. Will you make this right?

playerResponses.text: They murdered an innocent man. I'll hunt them down.
Justice and vengeance both pay the same. I'm in.
Vengeance is a dangerous road. I'll pass.

bindings.quest_action: track down whoever poisoned the well and make them answer
bindings.quest_focus: the poisoned well that killed three people
bindings.quest_stakes: justice for the dead and safety for the living
bindings.quest_threat: the deliberate poisoning of our water supply
bindings.quest_origin: months of escalating raids that led to this atrocity

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I'm going to be straight with you. I'm not asking for charity. I'm asking for justice.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Someone poisoned {quest_focus}. Not an accident: deliberate. Three people are dead, including {target_npc}'s daughter. The alchemist found traces of a compound from the caves {location_hint}, and the tracks belong to {enemy_type}. {quest_origin}, but {quest_threat} is something else entirely. This is war.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_stakes} demand that whoever ordered this answers for what they've done.

playerResponses.text: Poisoning a water supply is an act of war. I'll find whoever is responsible.
Tracking poisoners through caves sounds dangerous. What's the bounty?
This sounds like a feud that goes deeper than I want to get involved in.

bindings.quest_action: deliver the answer those murdered merchants' families deserve
bindings.quest_focus: the massacre site on the road
bindings.quest_stakes: the families waiting for people who will never come home
bindings.quest_origin: a senseless massacre that left goods untouched in the mud

dialogueChunks.intro: You look like someone who understands that some wrongs can't be forgiven, {player_name}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A merchant caravan was slaughtered on the road {location_hint} last week. Not robbed: slaughtered. The goods were left scattered, untouched. {target_npc} was the only survivor, hiding under a wagon. They saw {quest_threat}: {enemy_type} laughing while good people screamed. The guards filed a report. As if paperwork brings back the dead.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_stakes} deserve more than a report. Someone needs to {quest_action}.

playerResponses.text: Killing for sport? They won't be laughing when I find them.
I'll track them, but I need supplies and information first.
I hunt monsters, not ghosts. The trail is probably cold by now.

bindings.quest_action: shut down the crew that's been terrorizing the settlement
bindings.quest_focus: the extortion ring shaking down traders
bindings.quest_stakes: a sixteen-year-old apprentice who may never use his hand again
bindings.quest_threat: a violent crew that makes examples of anyone who resists

dialogueChunks.intro: Buy you a drink, {player_name}? Good. You'll need it after you hear what I have to say.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The blacksmith's apprentice was beaten half to death behind the tavern last night by {enemy_type} who've been running {quest_focus} for weeks. The boy refused to hand over the {quest_item} he'd been commissioned to deliver, so {quest_threat} made an example of him. {target_npc} pulled him out at sunrise. The guards know who's responsible: a crew holed up {location_hint}. But knowing and acting are two different things.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_stakes} because of {quest_origin}. If the guards won't act, someone else has to.

playerResponses.text: Show me where this crew is hiding. They'll answer for what they did.
Sixteen years old? I'll make them regret ever setting foot here.
Getting involved in local gang disputes isn't my fight.
